metadata.seed: Well, I'm working full time. I'm a part time student. I've just gotten engaged, and I live at home. There's just so much stress in my life. I have everything happening all at once. Just to top it all off, I failed an exam, a really big exam, and I just feel like I can't do anything anymore. I can't do anything right. I've just got so much stress, and I feel like such a disappointment, such a failure. And I've embarrassed my family by failing. I just don't know what to do anymore. I guess I've come here today to tell you, like, I want to leave uni, and I guess that's why I've come here to you today.

patient.query: Well, I'm working full time. I'm a part time student. I've just gotten engaged, and I live at home. There's just so much stress in my life. I have everything happening all at once. Just to top it all off, I failed an exam, a really big exam, and I just feel like I can't do anything anymore. I can't do anything right. I've just got so much stress, and I feel like such a disappointment, such a failure. And I've embarrassed my family by failing. I just don't know what to do anymore. I guess I've come here today to tell you, like, I want to leave uni, and I guess that's why I've come here to you today.

automatic_thoughts: I feel like such a disappointment

patient.query: Yeah, it definitely feels heavy. I guess, with everything going on, I just keep messing up. Like, I studied really hard for that exam, and then I didn’t do as well as I thought I would. It makes me feel like I’m not good enough, you know? I just wish I could get things right for once.

automatic_thoughts: I didn’t do as well as I thought I would
I wish I could get things right

patient.query: I think it’s just this voice in my head that keeps reminding me of all the times I’ve failed or let people down. It’s like, no matter how hard I try, there’s always this feeling that it’s just not enough. I start to doubt myself, and then I get anxious about even trying new things because I’m scared of failing again. It’s frustrating.

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

patient.query: It’s kind of a mix of things, really. Sometimes it tells me I’m going to embarrass myself, or that people are going to laugh at me. Other times, it just feels like it’s saying I’m not smart enough or talented enough to handle it. It makes me want to avoid those situations completely, like it’s easier to just stay in my comfort zone. But then I end up feeling even worse about myself.

automatic_thoughts: People are going to laugh at me

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)
Fear of being in a small group (finding)

patient.query: Sure, well, there was this time at work when I had to present a project in front of the team. I felt so confident in my work, but as soon as I started talking, I just froze up. I lost my train of thought, and I could see some people shifting in their seats. It was like my brain just shut down. Afterward, I replayed it over and over in my head, thinking about how everyone must have thought I was a mess. It made me

triggers: presenting a project in front of the team
automatic_thoughts: I lost my train of thought
everyone must have thought I was a mess
behaviors: replayed the event in my head

neutral: Ability to resist dangerous thoughts (observable entity)

patient.query: I think a friend would probably say that everyone has those moments, that I did my best, and that it’s okay to stumble sometimes. They’d probably remind me that it doesn’t define who I am. But then I can't help but think, “Would they really feel that way, or would they secretly be judging me?” It's hard to shake off that feeling, you know?

automatic_thoughts: Would they really feel that way, or would they secretly be judging me?

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: That’s a really insightful thought – it sounds like you’re already offering yourself a comforting perspective, and then questioning it. What makes you doubt that your friend would truly feel the way you described?

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds like you're really struggling with a lot of self-doubt, and it's understandable that you'd feel anxious when you question what your friend might think. It takes courage to consider what others might say, and even more to grapple with whether those thoughts are truly accurate.

socratic_questioning.response: That’s a really insightful thought – it sounds like you’re already offering yourself a comforting perspective, and then questioning it. What makes you doubt that your friend would truly feel the way you described?

safety_check.response: It sounds like you're experiencing a lot of anxiety, and it's tough when you're questioning whether your friends truly feel the way you'd like them to. When you said that, were you having any thoughts of hurting yourself or not wanting to be here?

patient.query: I guess it’s just this nagging thought that I’m not as likable as I want to be. Sometimes I feel like I’m putting on a show, trying to be someone I'm not. If they really knew the real me—like all my insecurities—they might not be as supportive. I can’t help but think that maybe people just put up with me rather than actually liking me. It’s hard to see it differently, even when I want to.

automatic_thoughts: I'm not as likable as I want to be
If they knew the real me, they might not be supportive
People just put up with me rather than actually liking me

neutral: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

llm_response.response:
Okay, it sounds like you're carrying a really difficult and persistent feeling that you're not as likable as you'd like to be. You've described a sense of putting on a show, a fear of being truly known, and a worry that people might not genuinely like you. It's understandable that this is hard to see differently, especially when you *want* to.

It takes courage to share these kinds of thoughts, and it sounds like they're causing you distress. Can you tell me a little more about what situations trigger these thoughts about being unlikeable?

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds like you're experiencing a lot of insecurity and worry about what others think of you, and that's really tough to carry around. It takes courage to share these kinds of feelings, and I appreciate you bringing them up.

patient.query: Yeah, it really does get heavy sometimes. I think social events are a big trigger for me—like parties or gatherings where I’m supposed to mingle. I worry about saying the wrong thing or not fitting in. I can feel my heart racing, and then I just end up overthinking everything. It’s like I go into this spiral, imagining how people might perceive me, and then I feel even more anxious. Sometimes I just want to hide away instead of facing that fear.

automatic_thoughts: worry about saying the wrong thing
imagining how people might perceive me

I end up picturing people whispering about me or rolling their eyes. Sometimes I even imagine their faces looking disappointed, and that just makes everything worse. It’s hard to shake off those feelings once they start.

what if I say something really stupid?

patient.query: It’s hard to say, honestly. I mean, sometimes I swear I see those looks on people's faces when I’m talking, like they’re bored or zoning out. But maybe it’s just my imagination, you know? It’s like I get stuck in my head and start reading into things that might not even be true. But in those moments, it feels so real, like I’m just a big disappointment.
